title: Conjecture 1.1 (Tomescu Conjecture)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a connected $k$-chromatic graph with $k \geq 4$. Then $G$ has at most $k!(k-1)^{|G|-k}$ $k$-colourings. Moreover, the extremal graphs are precisely the graphs obtained from $K_k$ by adding trees rooted at each vertex of the clique.
context_text: Tomescu conjectured in 1971 that the upper bound on the number of $k$-colourings attained by graphs formed from $K_k$ by growing trees at each vertex holds for any connected $k$-chromatic graph, for $k \geq 4$. The requirement $k \neq 3$ is necessary since odd cycles (and odd cycles with added trees) exceed the bound. Tomescu himself verified the conjecture for 4-chromatic planar graphs and proved a stronger $x$-colouring version for that class.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $k = 4$ in a strengthened form: every connected 4-chromatic graph $G$ on $n$ vertices satisfies $P_G(x) \leq x_4(x-1)^{n-4}$ for every integer $x \geq 4$, with equality for some (equivalently, every) $x \geq 4$ if and only if $G$ is obtained from $K_4$ by appending trees at each vertex of the clique.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled. The $k$-colouring bound $k!(k-1)^{|G|-k}$ is the falling-factorial formula $x_k(x-1)^{n-k}$ evaluated at $x=k$, consistent with equation (1) and (2) in the paper.
paper_title: Maximum number of colourings. I. 4-chromatic graphs
